title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Mrs. Clout asks Hercule Poirot to keep her visit a secret from her husband, Rolly Clout, specifically regarding a private matter they discussed. She expresses her belief in a higher purpose behind events, such as the coincidence of Robert Andraud being in the Valley of Wormesley. Poirot informs her that he intends to stay in Stagg for a few days, noting he was "guided" to St. Agatha by her own guidance.

Aunt Cassie discusses the upcoming marriage of Rolly Clout and Lynn Marchmont, noting Lynn's cleverness with numbers. She expresses concern that Lynn might become restless due to Rolly's limited outlook following his time on a farm during the war, potentially leading her toward someone more adventurous like David Hunter. She quickly denies any romantic understanding between Lynn and David, noting they often argue.

Dr. Leonnel Claude joins the conversation, appearing tired and haggard. He questions Poirot's decision to stay in Stagg, suggesting Poirot feels something is wrong with the case. Poirot agrees, expressing suspicion regarding the murder weapon. He suggests that while a pair of bloody tongs was found near the body, the wound might have been caused by a different, sharper object like a brick. Dr. Claude acknowledges the possibility of medical error and preconceived notions, noting that the victim was found face down on an Axminster carpet.

Aunt Katey enters with tea, and Dr. Claude leaves the room in dissatisfaction. Aunt Cassie laments Dr. Claude's poor mental state following the war and his inability to retire due to the death of Gordon, who died without a will. She finds comfort in the spirit world and the fact that Captain Porter successfully identified the murdered man as Robert Andrich.
